target.name: Cognition
target.founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 13 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Cognition is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Scott Wu, Steven Hao, and Walden Yan. The target has raised $175M+, positioning it in the upper tier of early-stage venture financings. The broader AI & Software sector comprises 1 public comparable (Palantir, PLTR) and 8 private peers including Waymo, Figure AI, Applied Intuition, and Skild AI, though none meet narrow same-stage and similar-valuation criteria for direct peer median analysis.

Only one valuation method is applicable to this target. The Stage-Progression approach uses total raised ($175M+) as a proxy for implied Seed-stage value of approximately $5M, then applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $12M. This method reflects observable venture progression patterns but does not leverage peer transaction multiples, as the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (fewer than 3 datapoints at narrow filters). A full-sector median across all 13 AI & Software comps would introduce material distortion given stage and scale heterogeneity.

The triangulated valuation range is $10M–$13M (median $12M), derived from Stage-Progression alone. This narrow derivation reflects methodological constraints rather than convergence across methods. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the 2.5x multiplier to this specific capital raise profile and the absence of near-peer transaction data to validate progression assumptions. Further evidence—including comparable Seed-stage financings in frontier AI or downstream revenue metrics—would be required to narrow the band.
